writing.author: Author

about.bio1: This site is the technical blog and portfolio of Dawood Saleh, a software engineer specializing in backend systems, serverless architecture, and AI tooling. Working independently with international clients since 2023, he builds backends on Cloudflare Workers, Telegram bots, and tools that connect AI models to real-world data.
about.bio2: The core focus is the Model Context Protocol (MCP) — the open standard that lets AI models call external tools and query live data sources. His MCP server for Arabic academic research opened AI-accessible access to millions of previously unindexed Arabic research records, and anchors a growing portfolio of MCP servers, automation bots, and serverless backends documented on this site.
about.bio3: Beyond the project work, this blog covers backend engineering, AI tooling, and the developer ecosystem forming around protocols like MCP — informed by a background that includes teaching Object-Oriented Programming at Hajjah University and writing technical research for academic and engineering audiences.

items.title: Arabic Academic Research MCP Server
items.description: MCP server integrating 4M+ records from Mandumah, ASJP, and SHAMAA — the first AI-accessible tooling for Arabic academic research. Published as an open tool for the Arabic research community.

items.title: Multi-Platform Telegram Media Bot
items.description: Telegram media downloader supporting 9+ platforms, deployed on Cloudflare Workers. Built with Node.js and Hono, featuring analytics tracking and cron-based automation.

items.title: Serverless Backend Systems
items.description: Architected serverless backends on Cloudflare Workers with KV storage, webhook automation, and scheduled tasks. Designed to be API-driven and maintenance-free.

contact.description: Open to backend engineering roles and serious freelance work. If the project involves AI tooling or serverless, that's my territory.
